label: About
title: About Chrome Glyph Observatory
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 68 recent saved-signal files and 79 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @songyou: Emi Takahashi is currently my favorite designer. she does incredible experiments... and Anchor image material 1, plus 7 more. A source image from framerusercontent.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Chrome Glyph Observatory: Create one finished still image as an architectural section using architectural section camera grammar: a single opened artifact spans the plate, cropped close and side-lit in cold blue flash. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against left black fiber edge opening, central rust-rimmed dark oval aperture, and upper-right circular chrome glyph aperture.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
